text.title: Tools, Services, and Gotchas
text.summary: CLI tools, SaaS services with agent-friendliness ratings, and runtime gotchas for AI accessibility automation.

section.title: CLI Tools

list.item: axe-core (via Playwright) — WCAG automated scanning with JSON output; npm install axe-core playwright
lighthouse CLI — Chrome-based a11y + performance audit; npm install -g lighthouse
pa11y-ci — Batch URL accessibility scanning with CI config; npm install -g pa11y-ci
wave-api — WebAIM WAVE API for page-level scanning; wave.webaim.org/api
ffmpeg — Video format preprocessing before caption submission

section.reference: pa11y-ci batch scanner with JSON output

list.item: Deque axe DevTools Enterprise — agent-friendly REST API + CLI; AI false-positive reduction; issue extraction with code context
Siteimprove Accessibility — agent-friendly REST API; continuous monitoring; webhook alerts for regression
3Play Media — agent-friendly REST API; AI-enabled captions + audio descriptions; ADA Title II workflow
Deepgram — agent-friendly REST API; ASR for caption generation; lower cost than 3Play; less editorial review
Level Access — partial API; "Ask Level AI" Q&A; VPAT generation; enterprise only
UserWay — agent-friendly scan API only; do NOT use overlay for compliance

antipattern.description: Running Playwright scanner without handling cookie consent banners and auth walls first.
antipattern.reason: Scanners that skip consent/auth produce incomplete results — they scan only the pre-auth or pre-consent state of the page.

antipattern.description: Scanning SPAs without explicit navigation triggers to all routes.
antipattern.reason: axe-core scans the current DOM snapshot only; routes not visited during the scan session are not audited.

antipattern.description: Using synchronous wait for caption API responses.
antipattern.reason: Caption APIs return files asynchronously; agents must implement polling with timeout and retry logic.

antipattern.description: Publishing AI-generated VPAT without human legal review.
antipattern.reason: LLM paraphrasing of WCAG criteria introduces compliance risk; VPAT criterion language is legally precise and must match W3C wording exactly.
